Description: Build reactive UI components with PHP classes and Blade views
Do: Keep components focused. Use typed properties. Separate concerns between component and view
Dont: Mix business logic directly in Blade views

Description: Use wire:model for two-way data binding between HTML inputs and component properties
Do: Use typed public properties. Apply validation rules. Use computed properties for derived data
Dont: Store everything in arrays without type safety
Code Bad: public array $form = [];\n// No type safety, no inline validation\n// $this->form['name'] = $request->name;

Description: Define user-triggered actions as public methods on Livewire components
Do: Keep actions focused. Validate before processing. Use wire:confirm for destructive actions
Dont: Put complex business logic directly in action methods

Description: Use events and listeners for parent-child and cross-component communication
Do: Use dispatch() and #[On] for event handling. Use Alpine.js for client-side events
Dont: Pass callbacks as properties or use global state

Description: Use wire:loading for UX feedback and optimize with lazy loading and polling
Do: Show loading indicators. Use lazy loading for heavy components. Debounce search inputs
Dont: Let users click buttons with no feedback during processing
Code Bad:
<button wire:click='processHeavyTask'>
    Process
    {{-- No loading state --}}
    {{-- User clicks multiple times --}}
</button>

Description: Use Livewire Form Objects to encapsulate form logic and validation
Do: Create Form classes for complex forms. Validate with rules. Use reset after submit
Dont: Define 20 public properties directly on the component

Description: Use Alpine.js for client-side interactivity that doesn't need server roundtrips
Do: Use @entangle for shared state. Use Alpine for modals tooltips and client-only interactions
Dont: Make server roundtrips for purely client-side interactions like toggling visibility
Code Good:
{{-- Alpine for UI state: --}}
{{-- Dropdown content --}}
{{-- Entangle with Livewire: --}}
"

Description: Test Livewire components with built-in testing utilities
Do: Use Livewire::test() for component testing. Assert properties renders and events
Dont: Only test the underlying models without testing component interactions
